title: Week 3 Day 5 (1/2) ‚ÄĒ Inference under the hood
summary: Part 1 of 2 ¬∑ Next-token loop ¬∑ temperature ¬∑ `visualizer.py` in Cursor.

sections.heading: Autoregressive generation loop
sections.remember: Each step: forward pass ‚Üí **vocab-sized** probability vector ‚Üí **pick one token** ‚Üí append to input ‚Üí repeat.
points: Streaming UI = one token at a time from this loop.
Reasoning models "think" by emitting tokens that later tokens must stay consistent with.
Not magic ‚ÄĒ statistical consistency over a long chain.

sections.heading: Temperature
sections.remember: **temperature=0** ‚Üí usually take **highest-probability** token (deterministic). Higher ‚Üí **sample** from distribution (more variety).
points: "More creative" ‚Čą more willing to pick non-top tokens.

sections.heading: `visualizer.py` (local, not Colab first)
sections.remember: Course **`week3/visualizer.py`** + **`day5.ipynb`** in **Cursor** ‚ÄĒ uses OpenAI **logprobs** to show top next tokens per step.
points: Prompt example: "Describe the color blue to someone who has never seen, in one sentence."
Step 1: **blue** ~100% ¬∑ then **is** vs **feels** ¬∑ chain builds poetic answer from greedy choices.
Proves frontier quality = strong next-token statistics, not a separate "truth module".
Try other prompts; reimplement with HF `generate` + logits for practice.

sections.heading: Logprobs API idea
sections.remember: You can request **top logprobs** per token from OpenAI ‚ÄĒ same loop you'd code manually with HF models.
points: Hands-on beats only hearing the theory.
Link to Week 1‚Äď2: typewriter effect = this loop exposed in the UI.

cheatSheet: Generate = repeat: forward ‚Üí pick token ‚Üí append
